oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Small ruminant dermatology – diseases, diagnostics and skin disease management
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Pucheu-Haston, Carie
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: Chorioptes
lice
zinc dermatitis
dermatophilosis
contagious ecthyma
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Dermatologic conditions represent an extremely common rea­son for clients to seek veterinary care for their animals regard­less of whether the animal is a pet or used for production for meat, milk, hide or fiber/wool. In contrast to large ruminants, small ruminants may easily fit into either category. Pet animals are typically presented to a veterinarian due to perception of patient suffering, illness or disfigurement. While these factors may also be important for production animals, dermatologic diseases may have significant impacts upon patient growth, fer­tility and quality of leather or fiber. The purpose of this lecture is to discuss the clinical appearance, diagnostic approach and treatment for six of the most common dermatologic conditions seen in goats, sheep and camelids. All of these conditions may be found in both pets and production animals; while the diag­nostic approach is often the same, the practical therapeutic ave­nues may need to be different. Where appropriate we will discuss factors that may complicate therapy, such as differences in sys­temic drug absorption or topical ectoparasiticide distribution.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 195-199
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
